firstSignalElement: Status badge flip FAILED->RETRYING + Errors&Jobs table move after Enter on 'Retry job'
confidenceStatement: PROTOCOL VIOLATION (reconciler downgrade): the tester's own hard give-up rule is 15 UI interactions / 3 exhausted strategies / 2 consecutive no-new-info interactions — task 1 ran to 46 interactions, roughly 3x the budget, before completing. Per RUN PROTOCOL this should have ended canComplete=no well before completion; the eventual success is compensation (persistence past the give-up line), not product ergonomics, so the reconciler scores this 'no' for gate purposes despite the tester's self-reported 'yes-with-struggle'. Genuine findings along the way remain valid: first Tab from page load skips ~11 header-region focusable elements landing on 'Refresh'; no first-time-user path from dashboard to a filtered failure view without a lucky pre-seeded 'Recent searches' shortcut; focus drops to <body> with zero focus-adjacent confirmation after the mutating Retry click.
wrongTurns: First Tab press did not land on the logo/nav/search — jumped to 'Refresh', skipping ~11 focusable header elements

ARIA tablist uses manual-activation: ArrowRight alone does not switch panels, Enter is additionally required

firstSignalElement: Scripted full scroll-through of the 513-row virtualized grid, row-index + instance-ID capture
confidenceStatement: All 513 AG-Grid row indices present with zero gaps; all 513 instance IDs unique; sort order held consistently across every Load-More seam checked — no duplicate/skipped-row confusion, backed by direct evidence not a guess.
protocolViolations: Several repeat 'Load more' clicks issued via browser_evaluate(button.click()) rather than the click tool, and grid-viewport scrolling driven via browser_evaluate rather than a documented UI affordance — compensatory verification technique, not a substitute for the real clicks already performed, but worth flagging per the 'agent compensatory behavior' protocol note
